content.text: Sound Editing

The EP-133 provides comprehensive sound editing capabilities including sample trimming, envelope shaping, filtering, pitch adjustment, and time stretching. Access edit mode by pressing SOUND/EDIT or holding SHIFT + SOUND.

content.caption: SOUND/EDIT button accesses sound selection and editing

content.text: Sound Selection
Press SOUND/EDIT to enter sound mode. Use the number keys to enter a sound number (0-999), then press ENTER to load that sound. You can also use +/- to browse through sounds sequentially.
Y Parameter
The black Y knob is context-sensitive and controls different parameters depending on which edit function is selected. After selecting a parameter with a number key, turn Y to adjust its value.

content.caption: Y knob adjusts the selected parameter

content.text: Edit Parameters
In edit mode (SHIFT + SOUND), the number keys access different parameters:

rows: Envelope attack time - how quickly sound reaches full volume
Envelope release time - how long sound fades after release
Stereo panning position - left to right placement
Low pass filter cutoff - removes high frequencies
High pass filter cutoff - removes low frequencies
Effect send - amount of signal sent to master effect
Time stretch - change length without changing pitch
Velocity sensitivity - how velocity affects volume/timbre

content.text: Sample Trimming
Trim the start and end points of your samples to remove silence or unwanted audio. In edit mode, use the Y knob to adjust trim points. The display shows the waveform position.
Envelope
Shape the amplitude envelope using Attack (ATK) and Release (REL) parameters. Attack controls the fade-in time, while Release controls the fade-out time after the pad is released.

content.title: Envelope Tips
content.text: Short attack + short release = punchy hits. Long attack = swells. Long release = ambient tails.
Filters

Two filter types are available: Low Pass Filter (LPF) removes high frequencies for a darker sound, while High Pass Filter (HPF) removes low frequencies for a thinner sound. Adjust cutoff frequency with the Y knob.

content.caption: Pads 4 and 5 access LPF and HPF in edit mode

content.text: Time Stretch
Time stretching allows you to change the length of a sample without affecting its pitch. This is useful for matching sample tempo to your project tempo, or for creative effects.

items: Press 9 (TIME) in edit mode to access time stretch
Use Y knob to adjust stretch amount
Stretch to tempo: match sample BPM to project BPM
Stretch to bar: fit sample to a specific bar length

content.text: Play Modes
Each sound can be set to different play modes that affect how it responds to pad presses:

items: One-shot: Sample plays fully when triggered, regardless of pad hold time
Gate: Sample plays while pad is held, stops when released
Legato: Overlapping triggers continue from current position instead of restarting

content.text: Committing Changes
After editing, hold MAIN/COMMIT to save your changes. If you want to discard changes and revert to the previous settings, simply press MAIN without holding.

content.title: Remember to Commit
content.text: Changes are not saved until you commit. Powering off without committing will lose your edits.
